calendar.matters:
Requesting Authority To Compromise Suit And For Authority To Implement Terms Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Nov-10-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Rosenberg for Gary Schefsky (Trustee).

Ms. Rosenberg stated that Lori Schefsky Clinton withdrew her objections and that she had not heard from Steven Schefsky. The Court granted the Petition Requesting Authority to Compromise Suit and for Authority to Implement Terms of Settlement Agreement (order for the same signed in open Court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Anasse.

Requesting Authority To Compromise Suit And For Authority To Implement Terms Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-13-2010 Continued To Nov-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Extraordinary Compensation Of Trustee And Confirming Actions Of Trustee Re: Winfield Litigation And Management
Off Calendar Oct-18-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Rosenberg for and with Gary Schefsky.

The Court stated that at the last hearing that it had recalled that Steve Schefsky appeared and stated that he had objections to the petitions. Ms. Rosenberg stated that they've had no contact with Steve Schefsky and that no objections have been filed. In addition, she stated that Lori does have counsel (Richard Johns), that in regard to this petition that Mr. Johns hasn't filed anything, that Mr. Johns had consented to the continuance of another petition which was currently set for 10/13/10, and that everything has been fully briefed. The Court stated that it would review the orders. Ms. Rosenberg stated that they had filed an amendment (to include the last quarter) which had been served and there was no objection to, that she submitted proposed orders for the Hyatt and Levin Folger petitions, that she had submitted an order with the amended amount, and that there is one proposed order confirming the Winfield litigation and one proposed order regarding the full injury and Hyatt litigation. The Court granted the petitions noting that it will refer to "fees" not "extraordinary fees" in the orders (subject to review of the orders). Ms. Rosenberg stated that she had revised the order to delete that language.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.

Petition For Order Approving Extraordinary Compensation Of Trustee And Confirming Actions Of Trustee Re Folger Levin Litigation And Hyatt Litigation
Off Calendar Oct-18-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Approving Extraordinary Compensation Of Trustee And Confirming Actions Of Trustee Re: Winfield Litigation And Management
Off Calendar Aug-04-2010 Continued To Oct-06-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Rosenberg for and with Gary Schefsky (Trustee). Steve Schefsky in Propria Persona.

Steve Schefsky stated that he was here to oppose the petition. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Gary Schefsky's request for extraordinary fees and the services he provided. The matter was continued to October 6, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Steve Schefsky to file a written objection which is due by September 15, 2010. Ms. Rosenberg's response is due by September 29, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Petition For Order Approving Extraordinary Compensation Of Trustee And Confirming Actions Of Trustee Re Folger Levin Litigation And Hyatt Litigation
Off Calendar Aug-04-2010 Continued To Oct-06-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Petition For Order Confirming Trusteeship
Off Calendar Apr-14-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Gary Schesfsky. Lori Schefsy Clinton in Propria Persona. Eileen Schefsky for and with Steve Schefsky.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positiions with regard to the Trustee's powers continuing post death, the interpleader action in Santa Clara, where the assets should be held, the partnerhip interest distribution and the issue as to who the Trustees are. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Svahlin.

Petition For Order Confirming Trusteeship
Off Calendar Apr-06-2009 Continued To Apr-13-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Gary Schefsky. Eileen Schefsky for Steve Schefsky. Lori Schefsky Clinton in propria persona.

Mr. Skootsky stated that he estimated that it would take the IRS approximately six months to do their review, that it was not unusual to get the "all clear" from the IRS before the distribution was done, and that once the family trusts were funded they would continue to be managed by his client; except with regard to the real estate. Eileen Schefsky stated that they disagreed; the siblings will manage their own trusts. Outside the presence of the Court, the parties attempted to agree on the form of the order. At the second call of the matter counsel stated that they could not agree. The matter was continued to April 13, 2009 to be heard before Commissioner Sue Kaplan.

Counsel presented a stipulation regarding the Safe Harbor petition (the hearing was held on March 23, 2009) which was signed in open court.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Application For Determination That S. Schefsky'S And L. Schefsky Clinton'S Opposition To G. Schefsky'S Petition For Order Confirming Trusteeship Will Not Invoke The No-Contest Clause In Trust.
Off Calendar Apr-06-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Gary Schefsky. Steve Schefsky in propria persona.

The Court stated a 21320 petition was filed by Steven Schefsky and his sister. Steven Schefsky stated his position for the record. The Court stated that his proposed opposition regarding the trusteeship needs to be filed under its own cover along with his 21320 petition. The Court then granted the 21320 petition (subject to a written order) subject to Steven Schefsky filing a proposed opposition with a cover. The Court finds that the opposition does not violate the no contest clause. Mr. Skootsky stated his position for the record. The Court continued the hearing on the petition to confirm trusteeship to April 6, 2009. Steve Schefsky is to prepare a proposed order granting the 21320 petition. In addition, the Court ordered that Steve Schefsky's proposed opposition is to be on file by the close of business on March 27, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Petition For Order Confirming Trusteeship
